Now, I want this to stand as living and television proof that anybody can learn to write creatively in just a few moments. A few moments ago, this woman was emotionally frozen, anal retentive, stuck in her abject, pathetic little world, and all of a sudden she has bloomed and throwered into an expressive, created person with the potential of june. Is that all not true, partner? Okay. You may think that was not a copywriting lesson, and you would be wrong. That not only is a copywriting lesson, it's a lesson in writing. One of the things, if you want to be a good copywriter or a good writer that you should do is push yourself to become more expressive in every way that there is to become expressive. If you can play a little bit of a musical instrument, you can do that. You can push yourself to tell a few jokes that aren't funny. You could get into dancing. Above all, you should write. You should do some writing every day. There's really nothing stopping you from being a good writer, except the governors that society and our parents and everybody has built into us all the time. And the thing is, you notice there's a different atmosphere in this seminar today than there was yesterday. That's always the case, because the people get moving. You get moving mentally. You're not as uptight and uninhibited. And it's very difficult not to be uptight and inhibited when you're sitting in front of a typewriter or when you have a legal patent and a pen in your hand. One of the things that I suggest that you do to get started when you're beginning to write is write Mike Tyson's telephone number, because that'll give you a little chuckle you'll get going and you'll be writing something. The biggest secret to getting good at writing is writing. That's not double talk. It's just really true. Okay. They're the ones that will keep doing it. If I do it, and I was just speaking to the amygdals, if I do it, it's not something that I would continue to do, but I could set up the training of others through it. Well, the idea is to do it so that you will not have to continue to do it. See, when I sold encyclopedias door to door, I was very, very good at it. But my income was extremely limited. It was limited by the amount of hours there are in the day. And no matter what, I could only knock on about 40 doors a night, and I could only make three or four presentations. Then I wrote a letter that made over 21, knocked on 21 million doors for me right away. What we have to do, this is a process that I call can it and clone it. And before you can clone it, you've got to can it. And so I personally think if you're the honcho of this organization that you should do it, but there's certainly no harm in having other people do it also. Now, what you want to do next is you want to go through this master piece collection of sales letters and find the one letter, and hopefully it won't be the one letter. You want to find the letters plural that are most likely to be a good template for your service. Oddly enough, that might not be another letter selling something that is healthy later. It may be something else. But you should find three or four of those letters. And then, and I know you won't like this, but this is the way it is. You need to sit down and you need to either type if you type or hand write out all of those letters. Just do it blindly. Stop trying to think about it. Now, as you do this process, thoughts will occur to you that specifically relate to your product or service. You should have another pad that can write to the right or left, depending on which hand it's on. To the right or left, that when those flashes come to you, you capture them on that other pad as you're writing them. Again, we're going to flash forward in time and pretend that you have done this. That you have already done every step that I have described and in your own handwriting, you have written out three or four good sales letters that are probably good templates for the sales letter that you need to write. At this point in time, you should back away from this job. You should leave it. No matter what ideas that you have or how good they are, you should not write them down or record them into a paper board. What you should do is keep them inside you and allow that tension of not being able to express it and build and build and build and build until you're almost ready to burst. And I promise you, if you do everything that I've just described, that will happen.
